puzzle.title: The Architect of Arrogance
puzzle.intro: A prominent figure in a powerful court, this individual was tasked with a monumental, yet blasphemous, project. Driven by loyalty to a tyrannical leader, their story is a stark warning about the heights of human pride and the inevitable fall that follows.
clues: I served in the court of a ruler who was blinded by power, and my counsel only deepened that darkness.
My name is forever linked with that of my leader and another wealthy contemporary, a trio of rebellion against the divine.
My expertise was in construction and administration, skills I used not for good, but for the grand designs of my master.
My leader commanded me to build a towering structure, a project born of pure arrogance.
The goal was audacious and foolish: to build a tower so high that it could reach the heavens and allow my master to see the God of Musa.
My master, Fir'awn, commanded me by name to kindle a fire upon the clay and make for him a lofty tower.

trial.label: What Was My Grand Project?
options: Building a lofty tower to the sky
Constructing a dam to divert a river
Erecting idols for a whole city
Forging a golden calf for worship
trial.answer: Building a lofty tower to the sky

location.label: What Was I Commanded to Use?

outcome.label: What Was Our Ultimate Fate?
options: We were drowned by the command of Allah
We were swallowed by the earth
We were destroyed by a mighty blast
We were granted forgiveness and guidance
outcome.answer: We were drowned by the command of Allah

puzzle.arabic: وَقَـٰرُونَ وَفِرْعَوْنَ وَهَـٰمَـٰنَ ۖ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَهُم مُّوسَىٰ بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ سَـٰبِقِينَ
puzzle.verse: And [We destroyed] Qārūn and Pharaoh and Hāmān. And Moses had already come to them with clear evidences, and they were arrogant in the land, but they were not outrunners [of Our punishment]. (29:39)
